Title: A Role for the Plastidial GPT2 Translocator in the Modulation of Lignin Biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem vascular tissues and interfascicular fibres
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GPT2 overexpression decreases stem lignin content, reducing both guaiacyl and syringyl lignin and associated monolignol pools, whereas GPT2 loss of function has no significant lignin phenotype.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPT2 is strongly induced in multiple lignin-defective backgrounds, especially HCT-RNAi stems. 2. Overexpression of GPT2 in Arabidopsis reduces total stem lignin and lowers G and S lignin units without changing H-unit content. 3. GPT2 overexpression reduces monolignol precursors and is associated with downregulation of HCT, 4CL2 and CSE. 4. gpt2 loss-of-function mutants show little effect on lignin but altered amino acid, TCA, and trehalose-related metabolite profiles. 5. Joint transcriptome-metabolome analysis suggests GPT2 helps balance carbon flux between lignin biosynthesis and other phenylpropanoid/light-protective metabolites plus primary metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA loss-of-function mutants (gpt2-1, gpt2-3); 35S overexpression in Col-0 and gpt2-1; qRT-PCR across insertion sites; lignin thioacidolysis; phloroglucinol staining; LC-MS/MS metabolomics; RNA-seq; joint pathway analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in stems of HCT-RNAi and multiple lignin-defective backgrounds; highly expressed in 35S-GPT2 lines; truncated transcripts in gpt2 mutants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stem tissue under standard growth conditions; comparisons across lignin-defective genotypes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: GPT2 overexpression reduced stem height; broad metabolic reprogramming included accumulation of nucleotide sugars, nucleotides, amino acids, and carbon-metabolism intermediates
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GPT2 is a plastidial glucose 6-phosphate:phosphate co-transporter. Loss of GPT2 alone did not change lignin, suggesting redundancy or conditional function. The study proposes GPT2 balances flux between lignin biosynthesis and light-protective phenylpropanoid derivatives. GPT1 was discussed as an essential paralog but was not a core gene of this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GPT2 is a candidate leverage point to lower lignin by rerouting carbon flux, but constitutive overexpression may cause height reduction and broad metabolic side effects; tissue-specific or inducible deployment may be preferable.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the hydroxycinnamoyl group from p-coumaroyl-CoA to shikimate in the monolignol pathway, enabling subsequent 3'-hydroxylation toward G and S lignin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem vascular tissues and interfascicular fibres
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced HCT expression lowers stem lignin and shifts composition toward increased H units with decreased G and S lignin; HCT is also downregulated in GPT2-overexpression lines, accompanying reduced G/S lignin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT-RNAi strongly reduced total lignin in Arabidopsis stems. 2. HCT downregulation increased H-lignin proportion while decreasing G and S lignin and their monolignol precursors. 3. In 35S-GPT2 lines, HCT transcript abundance was reduced, consistent with reduced G/S lignin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; stem lignin thioacidolysis; LC-MS/MS metabolomics; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Stem tissue; transcript reduced in HCT-RNAi and in Col-0[35S-GPT2] relative to WT
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation of G- and S-lignin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroponic growth, stem tissue analysis under standard long-day conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong HCT downregulation is associated with reduced stem height and severe reprogramming in earlier lines; lignin reduction can cause growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HCT is a core comparison gene and mechanistic target in this study, but the main experimental focus is the GPT2 transporter. The paper discusses cycling with shikimate and connection to the alternative APX1-mediated route from coumarate to caffeate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating HCT can reduce lignin but may incur growth penalties; coordinated tuning with GPT2 or related carbon-partitioning genes may help reduce lignin while moderating pleiotropic effects.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates coumaric acid to coumaroyl-CoA, supplying substrate for downstream lignin biosynthetic reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem vascular tissues and interfascicular fibres
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: 4CL2 was downregulated in 35S-GPT2 lines, consistent with reduced coumaroyl-CoA supply and reduced G/S lignin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL2 transcript levels were reduced in Col-0[35S-GPT2]. 2. Its downregulation was interpreted as limiting coumaroyl-CoA pools for lignin formation. 3. Reduced 4CL2 expression may explain why H-lignin-specific intermediates did not accumulate in 35S-GPT2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; pathway-focused transcript analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in vascular tissue for lignin biosynthesis; downregulated in Col-0[35S-GPT2]
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results specifically highlight 4CL2 transcript reduction in GPT2-overexpression lines as relevant to lignin changes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be combined with GPT2 modulation to tune lignin amount/composition through precursor supply control.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes caffeoyl shikimate to caffeic acid and shikimate in the lignin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem vascular tissues and interfascicular fibres
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CSE transcripts were reduced in 35S-GPT2, consistent with lower flux to G/S lignin precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSE was among the lignin-pathway genes downregulated in Col-0[35S-GPT2]. 2. This reduction aligned with decreased G and S monolignol pools. 3. CSE is part of the non-oxidative steps highlighted as sensitive to GPT2 overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; transcript heat map; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate required for G/S lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CSE is treated here as a mechanistically relevant pathway gene highlighted in the Results, though not the primary engineered gene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential partner target for lignin engineering when combined with carbon-partitioning genes such as GPT2.
